source_pr.title: executor, session, server/tests: stabilize audit retry reporting
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #66342

Problem Summary:

`TestAuditPluginRetrying` depended on unstable retry timing, and the completed audit event for no-result statements was emitted before retry bookkeeping finished. That could associate the retry signal with replayed internal executions instead of the final user statement.

### What changed and how does it work?

- emit the completed audit event from `FinishExecuteStmt`, after retry bookkeeping is settled
- preserve the outer statement context and retry count across `session.retry`
- make `TestAuditPluginRetrying` deterministic and assert the retry flag on the final user-visible statement path

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Executed locally:
- `go test -run '^(TestAuditPluginInfoForStarting|TestAuditPluginRetrying)$' -count=1 -tags=intest,deadlock ./pkg/server/tests/commontest`
- `go test -run '^TestPrepareAndCompleteSlowLogItemsForRules$' -count=1 -tags=intest,deadlock ./pkg/executor`
- `make lint`

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Retry counts are now accumulated and preserved so slow-query and audit logs reflect the final retry state; statement summaries no longer over-count optimistic autocommit retries and respect configured retry limits.

* **Tests**
  * Added deterministic tests validating retry-count reporting, retry-limit enforcement, and isolated statement-summary metrics.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

symptoms: - make `TestAuditPluginRetrying` deterministic and assert the retry flag on the final user-visible statement path | // Usually the following retry-loop will succeed in the first try. However, if we are lucky | // enough, it might need more times to trigger the retry. | require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
failure_signature: - make `TestAuditPluginRetrying` deterministic and assert the retry flag on the final user-visible statement path | // Usually the following retry-loop will succeed in the first try. However, if we are lucky | // enough, it might need more times to trigger the retry. | require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
root_cause_explanation: Audit retry reporting was unstable because the completed audit event for no-result statements could be emitted before retry bookkeeping finished, causing retry flags/counts to be associated with internal retries instead of the final user statement. The PR emits the completed event after retry bookkeeping and preserves the outer statement context/retry count, making the test deterministic without relying on timing.

analysis:
PR title: executor, session, server/tests: stabilize audit retry reporting
PR url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/67308
Triage sources: manual_triage, determinism_stabilization
Triage summary: - make `TestAuditPluginRetrying` deterministic and assert the retry flag on the final user-visible statement path | // Usually the following retry-loop will succeed in the first try. However, if we are lucky | // enough, it might need more times to trigger the retry. | require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
Changed files (5): pkg/executor/adapter.go, pkg/executor/adapter_test.go, pkg/server/tests/commontest/tidb_test.go, pkg/session/session.go, pkg/session/tidb_test.go
Diff highlights (excerpt):
- ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, plugin.IsRetryingCtxKey, a.retryCount > 0 || sessVars.RetryInfo.Retrying)
- ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, plugin.IsRetryingCtxKey, sessVars.StmtCtx.ExecRetryCount > 0 || sessVars.RetryInfo.Retrying)
- sessVars.StmtCtx.ExecRetryCount += uint64(a.retryCount)
- sessVars.StmtCtx.ExecRetryCount = uint64(a.retryCount)
- stmtExecInfo.ExecRetryCount = a.retryCount
- stmtExecInfo.ExecRetryCount = uint(sessVars.StmtCtx.ExecRetryCount)
- // Usually the following retry-loop will succeed in the first try. However, if we are lucky
- // enough, it might need more times to trigger the retry.
Full patch analyzed; see patch_url.
